qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested brace expressions crash minimatch's shipped brace-expansion path
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a stack-exhaustion path in braceExpand() and the shipped brace-expansion dependency, not the advisory's repeated-* pattern compilation flaw that generates too many [^/]*? regex groups and backtracks exponentially on a missing literal.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Deeply nested extglobs crash minimatch during pattern compilation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding reaches recursive extglob parsing in AST.fromGlob() and overflows the call stack, whereas the advisory concerns adjacent non-globstar * characters in AST.#parseGlob() compiling into a regex that backtracks exponentially when a later literal is absent.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26 is the repeated-wildcard ReDoS fixed by coalescing adjacent non-globstar * characters in src/ast.ts, while the manifest-backed qualifying findings concern brace-expansion recursion and extglob recursion.
evidence: The advisory patch 2e111f3 modifies src/ast.ts so consecutive non-globstar '*' characters collapse into one regex fragment instead of emitting one '[^/]*?' group per star.

nested_brace_expansion_stack_overflow reports and reproduces RangeError from braceExpand() delegating into brace-expansion recursion; it does not involve the advisory's missing-literal backtracking condition or the patched consecutive-* logic.
nested_extglob_stack_overflow reports and reproduces RangeError from deeply nested @(...) parsing in AST.fromGlob(); that is a recursion-depth bug, not the advisory's regex backtracking path.
